'''Mitchell Pearce''' (born 7 April 1989) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for [[Sydney Roosters]], with whom he won the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[NRL]]. Pearce has played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]], [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]], the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] and the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] side.

==Early life==
Pearce was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, [[Australia]]. He is the son of the ARL commissioner, former [[Balmain Tigers]] legend, [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] and [[Australian national rugby league team|Australian]] international [[Wayne Pearce]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/im-proud-of-him-wayne-pearce-on-the-journey-of-blues-no7-mitchell-pearce-20170619-gwtuih.html|title=I'm proud of him: Wayne Pearce on the journey of Blues No.7 Mitchell Pearce|first=Michael|last=Chammas|date=20 June 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/commissioners/wayne-pearce-oam/|title=Wayne Pearce OAM – NRL|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>
 
He played his junior football for the [[Asquith Magpies]] and [[North Ryde Hawks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://myaccount.news.com.au/sites/dailytelegraph/subscribe.html?sourceCode=DTWEB_WRE170_a&mode=premium&dest=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/wallaby-kurtley-beale-admits-his-state-of-origin-dreams-and-admiration-for-greg-inglis/news-story/37e83fc46ded926772ede7d0c2bfb4aa?nk=ee400f6e0bcf201c1768e0c16046452d-1554711263&memtype=anonymous|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=myaccount.news.com.au|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce attended and played [[rugby union]] for [[Barker College]], before transferring to [[Marist Catholic College North Shore]] for his senior years, where he played league alongside his friend [[Kieran Foran]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/pearce-has-touch-of-the-legends/news-story/a97d6339c6577c8812d948fa1c672c3d|title=Pearce has touch of the legends|date=27 March 2010|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/blood-brothers-geared-up-for-the-biggest-fight-of-their-lives-20131004-2uzoz.html|title=Blood brothers geared up for the biggest fight of their lives|first=Glenn|last=Jackson|date=4 October 2013|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce would later be signed by the Sydney Roosters.

==Playing career==
===Sydney Roosters (2007-2017)===
In Round 2 of the [[2007 NRL season]], Pearce made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], starting at halfback in the 43–6 loss at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/cowboys-roll-roosters/2007/03/24/1174597964537.html|title=Cowboys roll Roosters – smh.com.au|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=25 March 2007|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> After three First Grade appearances for the Roosters, Pearce's contract was extended by the club to the end of the 2010 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,21544133-23214,00.html|title=FOX SPORTS – Live Sports Scores – NRL, AFL, Cricket Scores|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In Round 12 against the [[Newcastle Knights]], Pearce scored his first NRL career try in the Roosters 22–18 loss at [[Central Coast Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/knights-bounce-back-with-thrilling-win/2007/06/02/1180205574342.html|title=Knights bounce back with thrilling win – smh.com.au|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2 June 2007|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In Round 14 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Pearce scored an 80-metre try in the final minute from a loose carry from Panthers halfback [[Craig Gower]], the man who previously drunkenly attacked Pearce 2 years prior and got the last laugh by scoring the try of his mistake to win the match for the Roosters 24–20 at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/roosters-claim-lastminute-win/2007/06/16/1181414612751.html|title=Roosters claim last-minute win – smh.com.au|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=16 June 2007|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Later into the season, Pearce was ruled out for 6 weeks to due a back injury{{cn|date=November 2024}} and later in Round 23 onwards where he played off the interchange bench in last 3 matches. Pearce finished his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 16 matches and scoring 2 tries in the [[2007 NRL season]].

In Round 21 of the [[2008 NRL season]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], Pearce scored his [[hat trick]] of tries in the Roosters 32–20 win at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2008-08-02/roosters-return-from-the-brink-to-down-cowboys/462206|title=Roosters return from the brink to down Cowboys|publisher=ABC News|date=2 August 2008|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> 
[[File:Mitchellpearce.jpg|thumb|right|Pearce in 2008]]

Pearce finished the 2008 season with him playing in 25 matches, scoring 7 tries and kicking 1 field goal for the Roosters.

Pearce finished Roosters' [[Australian rugby league wooden spooners|wooden spoon]] dreaded [[2009 NRL season]] with him playing in all the Roosters 24 matches, scoring 8 tries and kicking 1 field goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/the-wooden-spoon-to-premier-gold-roosters-run-recalls-league-history-20100921-15lfu.html|title=The wooden spoon to premier gold? Roosters run recalls league history|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=21 September 2010|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

Pearce would later be one of the main players to lead the Roosters who were last place wooden spooner's in the previous season to the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], as the Roosters made the finals on 6th place.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/wooden-spoon-to-grand-final-for-sydney-roosters/news-story/5cb014a024185f5212327ff385b4e5ea|title=Wooden spoon to grand final|date=24 September 2010|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/bornagain-roosters-smash-titans-dream-20100924-15qn0.html|title=Born-again Roosters smash Titans dream|first=Phil|last=Lutton|date=24 September 2010|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> On 3 October 2010, Pearce started at halfback in the Roosters 32–8 defeat on the big day.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/dragons-v-roosters-in-nrl-grand-final/news-story/ca911495e4b4e67c9bae9c28d8c9ffd5|title=Dominant Dragons win NRL final|date=3 October 2010|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce finished the [[2010 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches and scored 9 tries for the Roosters.

In Round 18 of the [[2011 NRL season]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Pearce played his 100th NRL career match in the Roosters 34–26 win at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/live-roosters-v-panthers/news-story/868b54c862055933c413db372b0974ab|title=Brave Mitch saves Rooster mates|date=10 July 2011|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce finished the season with him playing in 21 matches, scoring 3 tries and kicking 1 field goal.

In June 2012, Pearce was rumoured to leave the Roosters to join the [[Wests Tigers]] because a tough relationship with the [[Brian Smith (rugby league, born 1954)|Brian Smith]] over him being printed on the papers due to his party boy antics.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-rocked-by-rift-between-mitchell-pearce-and-brian-smith/news-story/017b39381370e4a0805ab617f6867fc5|title=Smith, Pearce near breaking point|date=21 July 2012|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-looking-to-poach-mitchell-pearce/news-story/50c65eab9713859c032569cc349a02bb|title=Pearce is in eye of the Tigers|date=9 June 2012|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce finished the [[2012 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches and scoring 10 tries.

On 13 February 2013, Pearce extended his contract with the Roosters to the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/sydney-roosters-give-halfback-mitchell-pearce-four-year-extension/news-story/cb1536447f8be60fb7ea4a5d9b9e5ea9|title=Roosters give Pearce four-year extension|date=12 February 2013|website=The Australian|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> With a new coach of [[Trent Robinson]] and new halves partner of [[James Maloney (rugby league)|James Maloney]], Pearce's form was improving from previous seasons and later played in the [[2013 State of Origin series]], playing in all 3 matches at halfback alongside Maloney in the 2–1 series loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/big-brother-james-maloney-will-be-watching-over-mitchell-pearce-in-origin-i/news-story/b5da20d3dd0d7b895752658b55a93983|title=Big brother watching over Pearce|date=4 June 2013|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> In Round 23 against the [[Wests Tigers]], Pearce played his 150th NRL career match and scored 2 tries in the 56–14 win at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/sport/nrl/pearce-will-no-longer-let-the-critics-get-him-down-20130817-2s3zv.html|title=Pearce will no longer let the critics get him down|first=Andrew|last=Webster|date=17 August 2013|website=Brisbane Times|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sydney-roosters-embarrass-terrible-wests-tigers/news-story/9eb8e1ccda933fcea448eb5db6536376|title=Roosters embarrass terrible Tigers|date=19 August 2013|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce would help the Roosters to win the Minor Premiership and later made it into the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] where they played against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-season-that-was-for-minor-premiers-sydney-roosters-on-their-road-to-the-nrl-finals-series/news-story/93b5cc70d1bf279395f177a1e9698890|title=Road to the finals: Roosters|date=10 September 2013|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> On 6 October 2013, Pearce played at halfback in the 26–18 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/rolling-coverage-of-sydney-roosters-v-manly-sea-eagles-nrl-grand-final/news-story/62432b5368e240c000708375b77e4bca|title=Tri time! Roosters win epic|date=6 October 2013|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/74996/default.aspx|title=NRL Grand Final player ratings|date=6 October 2013|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce finished the Roosters successful [[2013 NRL season]] with him playing in 26 matches and scoring 5 tries.

In May 2014, Pearce was given an infringement notice by police after he refused to leave a nightclub in [[Kings Cross, New South Wales|Kings Cross]] after a female clubber complained about his actions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/mitchell-pearce-arrested-after-kings-cross-nightclub-altercation/news-story/b86a52c981d0f432a08fc68d3f74edc7|title=NRL star held after nightclub incident|date=11 May 2014|website=The Australian|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> He was later fined $20,000 and given a 1 match suspension.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters/roosters-halfback-mitchell-pearce-fined-and-suspended-following-nightclub-altercation/news-story/73ef7f11c4f87e1c0c30aa74960a7ad5|title=Pearce stood down, woman revealed|publisher=News.com.au |date=13 May 2014|access-date=9 March 2018|last1=Massoud|first1=Josh}}</ref> Pearce was later dropped from the New South Wales squad, where [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] halfback [[Trent Hodkinson]] took over his incumbent spot and by coincidence he scored the winning try in Game 2 to win the series for the Blues for the first time since 2005 as Pearce has never had an Origin series win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/why-mitchell-pearce-was-dropped-from-nsw-origin-team-20140520-zrj2x.html|title=Why Mitchell Pearce was dropped from NSW Origin team|first=Brad|last=Walter|date=20 May 2014|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/trent-hodkinson-joins-list-of-unlikely-origin-heroes-20140619-zse40.html|title=Trent Hodkinson joins list of unlikely Origin heroes|first=Michael|last=Carayannis|date=19 June 2014|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce finished the [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 26 matches and scoring 9 tries for the Roosters.

In Round 21 of the [[2015 NRL season]] against the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs, Pearce became the youngest player in NRL history to play 200 career matches (at 26 years 115 days) as the Roosters won 38–28 at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/roosters-mitchell-pearce-to-play-200th-nrl-game-in-defiance-of-the-critics-looking-to-shoot-him-down/news-story/f3c693ea0fbb37b6fff1aa7204ea6130|title=Pearce defies critics to hit 200|date=30 July 2015|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/07/31/roosters-prevail-in-friday-night-classic/|title=Roosters prevail in Friday night classic|date=31 July 2015|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce was appointed co-captain of the Roosters and his side went on to win the Minor Premiership to bowed out in the Preliminary Final against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] when they were defeated 31–12 at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/jake-friend-and-mitchell-pearce-named-roosters-cocaptains-20150216-13g2cq.html|title=Jake Friend and Mitchell Pearce named Roosters co-captains|first=Steve|last=Zemek|date=16 February 2015|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/brisbane-broncos-blitz-sydney-roosters-3112-to-charge-into-2015-nrl-grand-final/news-story/a120098990c856e832518ff32ae68544|title=Roosters rolled by brilliant Broncos|date=25 September 2015|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce finished the [[2015 NRL season]] with him playing in 21 matches and scoring 1 try for the Roosters.

After Pearce's controversial drunken Australia Day antics in January 2016 that were caught on video, Pearce was fined $125,000 by the NRL, suspended for 8-matches and lost his co-captaincy for the Roosters.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/sydney-roosters-star-mitchell-pearce-banned-for-8-weeks-120k-fine-over-lewd-dog-video/news-story/8d1083036139156143cbb3b0999fa93f|title=Pearce banned EIGHT weeks, $125k fine|date=3 March 2016|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce later made his return for the Roosters in Round 8 against the [[Newcastle Knights]], scoring a try in the 38–0 win at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/04/30/pearce-return-helps-roosters-to-easy-win/|title=Pearce return helps Roosters to easy win|date=30 April 2016|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce was not selected for New South Wales for the [[2016 State of Origin series]], with selectors opting to put [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] half [[Adam Reynolds]] at halfback.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2016-mitchell-pearce-keen-to-be-overlooked-by-laurie-daley-for-series-20160506-gonpx7.html|title=State of Origin 2016: Mitchell Pearce keen to be overlooked by NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley|date=5 May 2016|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/07/07/robinson-backs-pearce-for-origin-return/|title=Robinson backs Pearce for Origin return|date=6 July 2016|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce finished his rough [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in a career low 12 matches and scoring 1 try for the Roosters.

In the Round 8 of the [[2017 NRL season]] during the [[Anzac Day]] match against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], Pearce kicked a game winning field goal in the 83rd minute during Golden Point to give the Roosters a 13–12 victory and won the ANZAC Man of the Match Medal at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/mitchell-pearce-kicks-roosters-to-1312-victory-over-dragons-in-anzac-day-clash-at-allianz-stadium/news-story/0405373d532c57eec429e2ea00b04337|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In Round 16 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], Pearce would again kick a match winning field goal in Golden Point to give the Roosters a 25–24 victory at [[Adelaide Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/mitchell-pearce-nails-golden-point-field-goal-as-roosters-stun-storm-2524/news-story/9bafe98a16695886dfda559d5294d22c|title=Pearce magic seals stunning comeback|date=24 June 2017|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce who started the season in good form would again get selected to play at halfback for New South Wales for the [[2017 State of Origin series]] in all 3 matches and scored 2 tries in another unsuccessful attempt for Pearce to be part of winning Origin team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2017-mitchell-pearce-says-somethings-wrong-with-nsw-preparation-20170722-gxgk0s.html|title=State of Origin 2017: Mitchell Pearce says 'something's wrong' with NSW preparation|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=22 July 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/has-mitchell-pearce-played-his-last-state-of-origin-game-20170712-gxa1mf.html|title=State of Origin 2017, Game Three: Has Mitchell Pearce played his last State of Origin game?|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=12 July 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> On 27 September 2017, Pearce was the winner of the [[Peter Frilingos]] Headline Moment of the Year of his match winning performance in the Roosters ANZAC Day clash against the St George Illawarra Dragons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://myaccount.news.com.au/sites/dailytelegraph/subscribe.html?sourceCode=DTWEB_WRE170_a&mode=premium&dest=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/dally-m-awards-2017-mitchell-pearces-anzac-day-winner-claims-headline-moment/news-story/ca4742e38359f287907e55eb3d99de43?nk=80620af520cbebc834dac5dd22e6bd06-1554711354&memtype=anonymous|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=myaccount.news.com.au|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Following the 2017 season in which Pearce helped the Roosters take 2nd place on the ladder and made it to the preliminary final, it was announced that the Roosters had signed [[Melbourne Storm]] superstar halfback [[Cooper Cronk]] on a two-year deal. Pearce, feeling betrayed, started weighing his future in what would be the biggest decision in his career.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/cooper-cronk-admits-mitchell-pearce-has-right-to-be-upset-over-roosters-playmaker-reshuffle/news-story/7670ae314dd87cece0e41b9860747c6f|title=Mitchell Pearce Sydney Roosters NRL 2018: Cooper Cronk admits sympathy for pressured halfback|date=31 October 2017|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In the end, Pearce decided to leave the club and on 20 November 2017, the Sydney Roosters granted Pearce an immediate release from his remaining contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/cronulla-manly-and-newcastle-are-now-locked-in-a-threeway-battle-for-mitchell-pearce/news-story/6b394c1eed02253dc8d2c9f32d4f9c79|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce was negotiating with the [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/mitchell-pearce-has-requested-an-immediate-release/news-story/9c9e0c8b2fe830b8a484015c30f73e07|title=Roosters sign Mitchell Pearce release|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> On 30 November 2017, it was announced that Pearce signed a 4-year deal with the Newcastle Knights starting from 2018, worth about $4 Million over the time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/knights/newcastle-knights-reportedly-win-battle-for-mitchell-pearce-signature-on-fouryeardeal/news-story/23677eb6ef49478cd79534eff0700a60|title=Mitchell Pearce signs with Newcastle Knights: Manly, Cronulla Sharks lose battle|date=30 November 2017|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2018-newcastle-knights-win-the-race-to-sign-sydney-roosters-halfback-mitchell-pearce-20171130-gzvwsf.html|title=NRL 2018: Newcastle Knights win race to sign halfback Mitchell Pearce|first=Robert|last=Dillon|date=30 November 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce finished his 10-year stint with the Sydney Roosters with him playing in playing in 23 matches, scoring 7 tries and kicking 3 field goals in the 2017 NRL season.

===Newcastle Knights (2018-2021)===
In round 1 of the [[2018 NRL season|2018 season]], Pearce made his debut for the Knights in their 19–18 [[golden point]] extra-time win over the Manly Warringah Sea Eagles, kicking the winning [[Drop goal#Rugby league|field goal]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2018/03/09/late-mail-knights-v-manly/|title=Late mail: Knights v Manly|date=9 March 2018|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In round three Pearce played against his former club Sydney Roosters going down 8–38. 4 weeks later in the round 7 clash he tore his pectoral muscle in the Knights' 22–20 win against the [[Wests Tigers]] at Scully Park. This Injury caused him to miss 10 weeks where in that time, the Knights only won 2 games. Once returning from injury, Pearce played in the remaining 8 games of the season. He ended the year with 22 votes in the [[Dally M Awards]], finishing in 5th overall and 2nd for the Knights.{{cn|date=November 2024}}

Pearce played 21 games for Newcastle in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club reached the finals for the first time since 2013. Pearce played in the club's elimination final loss against South Sydney.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/01/10/knights-stat-to-fix-second-half-comebacks/|title=Newcastle to fix second half comebacks|publisher=National Rugby League|date=9 January 2021}}</ref>

In January 2021, Pearce stepped down as Newcastle captain after he allegedly sent text messages of a sexual nature to a female Newcastle staff member.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/newcastle-knights-mitchell-pearce-texting-saga-blake-green-tyson-frizell/95bc40f2-edaf-4ae9-9ee2-8a058df77481/|title=How Knights' recruitment can salvage 2021 season from Mitchell Pearce mess|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

In round 3 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he played his 300th first grade game in Newcastle's loss against the [[Wests Tigers]]. The following week, Pearce was taken from the field with a pectoral injury in Newcastle's defeat to [[St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/tariq-sims-classy-gesture-in-heated-nrl-clash/news-story/bd503a1aecc29017f25d1dc1c2fd72d2|title=Tariq Sims' classy gesture in heated NRL clash|publisher=News.com.au |date=4 April 2021|last1=Bruce|first1=Jasper}}</ref>

In round 24, Pearce kicked a field goal for Newcastle in the final minute of play which won the game for the club 15–14 over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].  The victory meant Newcastle would qualify for the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/08/26/knights-pip-titans-by-a-point-to-secure-finals-berth/|title='Champions stand up in big moments': O'Brien hails match-winner Pearce|publisher=National Rugby League|date=26 August 2021}}</ref>

On 27 October, Pearce requested a release from the final year of his Newcastle contract to join [[Super League]] side [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2022.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-10-27/nrl-mitchell-pearce-seeks-newcastle-knights-contract-release/100573980/|title=Newcastle Knights confirm Mitchell Pearce wants immediate release from NRL contract|publisher=ABC News|date=27 October 2021}}</ref> The request release was granted on 15 November.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2021/11/15/statement-mitchell-pearce/|title=Statement: Mitchell Pearce|date=14 November 2021|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===Catalan Dragons (2022-2023)===
In round 1 of the [[2022 Super League season]], Pearce made his club debut for [[Catalans Dragons]] where they lost 28–8 against [[St Helens R.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-beat-catalans-in-fiery-season-opener-talking-points/|title=St Helens beat Catalans in fiery season opener – talking points|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=10 February 2022}}</ref>

Pearce finished the 2022 season scoring ten tries in 23 games. Pearce played in Catalans elimination final loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62813693|title=Super League play-offs: Catalans Dragons 10–20 Leeds Rhinos – Liam Sutcliffe hat-trick secures win|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 12 September 2023, Pearce announced that he would retire from rugby league following the conclusion of the [[2023 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/news-2023-mitchell-pearce-announces-retirement-sydney-roosters-newcastle-knights-catalans-dragons/be13477e-ab5f-4a1b-8590-f1d52083c428/|title=Sydney Roosters premiership winner Mitchell Pearce announces retirement from rugby league|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>
On 14 October, Pearce played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] which would also be his final match as a player.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Representative career===
On 2 May 2008, Pearce made his representative debut, playing for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]] against [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country Origin]] and starting at halfback in the 22–22 all draw at [[WIN Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/country-city-draw-22-22/news-story/ceb8e3b859a606d1531bb8851175a131|title=Country, City draw 22–22|date=1 May 2008|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> On 2 July 2008, Pearce was selected to make his [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] debut for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]], replacing the injured [[Peter Wallace (rugby league)|Peter Wallace]] in the Game 3 series decider match at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/archive/news/pearce-anasta-paired-for-blues/news-story/c4bc2ba87ab4b3c1c250a8703c740192|title=Pearce, Anasta paired for Blues}}</ref> The Blues were defeated 16–10 and lost the series.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2008-07-02/state-of-origin-iii-2008---the-decider/2492650|title=State of Origin III, 2008 – the Decider|date=2 July 2008|publisher=ABC News|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce later said, "In hindsight I wish I never played that early when I was 19 to 22. I definitely wasn't ready. As a halfback in Origin at 19, it was way too early. I probably developed some bad doubts from that period in those first few games. It's a daunting thing."<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=State of Origin: Mitchell Pearce opens up about his demons and his regret NSW first selected him at 19| author=Michael Chammas| url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-mitchell-pearce-opens-up-about-his-demons-and-his-regret-nsw-first-selected-him-at-19-20150613-ghmwy9|access-date=14 June 2015|date=13 June 2015}}</ref>

Pearce regained the halfback spot for New South Wales in Game 2 of the [[2010 State of Origin series]], where they were beaten by Queensland 34–6 at [[Suncorp Stadium]] and lost the series in a fifth consecutive year.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2010-06-10/barrett-ready-to-rock-with-pearce/862308|title=Barrett ready to rock with Pearce|publisher=ABC News|date=10 June 2010|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/queensland-v-nsw-in-state-of-origin-ii-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/62656354491d5a75b13adb48901abb43|title=Fantastic five for magic Maroons|date=16 June 2010|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce also played in Game 3, where the Blues were beaten 23–18 at [[ANZ Stadium]] and were beaten in a whitewash 3–0 series loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nsw-v-queensland-in-state-of-origin-3-at-anz-stadium/news-story/8fd8c4343f1d15dc130effd088fe8056|title=Maroons clinch clean sweep|date=7 July 2010|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref>

On 6 May 2011, Pearce played for City Origin against Country Origin, playing at halfback in the 18–12 loss in [[Albury]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.adelaidenow.com.au/sport/nrl/country-beating-city-at-half-time/news-story/b4be85989e326e420de050cec6f6f8db?sv=5367ac94853ca8e2492f52878be13d12&nk=74057c9e9b98ffe704d6a984f7c988a1-1554711300|title=Pearce stakes claim, Soward hurt|date=6 May 2011|website=The Advertiser|location=Adelaide|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref> Pearce was selected to play at halfback in all 3 matches for New South Wales in their 2–1 series defeat in the [[2011 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/jul/06/queensland-new-south-wales-state-origin|title=Queensland 34–24 New South Wales – State of Origin match report|agency=Press Association|date=6 July 2011|website=The Guardian|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce scored his first Origin try in Game 1, where the Blues lost 16–12 at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8536478/Queensland-16-New-South-Wales-12-match-report.html|title=Queensland 16 New South Wales 12: match report|first=Telegraph staff and|last=agencies|date=25 May 2011|access-date=9 March 2018|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London}}</ref> On 25 September 2011, Pearce played for [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], playing at halfback in the 36–22 win in [[Lae]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2011/09/05/kangaroos-train-on-squad-named/|title=Kangaroos train-on squad named|date=5 September 2011|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

On 22 April 2012, Pearce played for City Origin against Country Origin, playing at halfback in the 24–22 loss in [[Mudgee, New South Wales|Mudgee]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2012-04-22/city-survives-country-comeback/3965262|title=City survives Country comeback|publisher=ABC News|date=22 April 2012|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> Pearce was selected to play at halfback in all 3 matches for New South Wales in their 2–1 series defeat in the [[2012 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/cronk-seals-maroons-victory-in-fullthrottle-emotional-finale-20120705-21i0d.html|title=Cronk seals Maroons victory in full-throttle, emotional finale|first=Phil|last=Lutton|date=4 July 2012|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

In 2013, Pearce's form was improving from previous seasons and later played in the [[2013 State of Origin series]], playing in all 3 matches at halfback alongside [[James Maloney (rugby league)|James Maloney]], in the 2–1 series loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/big-brother-james-maloney-will-be-watching-over-mitchell-pearce-in-origin-i/news-story/b5da20d3dd0d7b895752658b55a93983|title=Big brother watching over Pearce|date=4 June 2013|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref>

Pearce made his return to the Origin arena for the [[2015 State of Origin series]] playing at five-eighth, a position he usually does not play at in all 3 matches as NSW went down to lose series 2–1.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2015-mitchell-pearce-relieved-michael-jennings-dodged-nsw-blues-axe-20150630-gi1ntq.html|title=State of Origin 2015: Mitchell Pearce relieved Michael Jennings dodged NSW Blues axe|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=30 June 2015|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2015-mitchell-pearce-will-deliver-for-nsw-writes-jarryd-hayne-20150524-gh8frf.html|title=State of Origin 2015: Mitchell Pearce will deliver for NSW, writes Jarryd Hayne|first=Jarryd|last=Hayne|date=24 May 2015|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref> In Game 3 as the Blues copped an embarrassing 52–6 series decider defeat, Pearce was on the receiving off a brutal sledge from Queensland five-eighth [[Johnathan Thurston]], saying to Pearce, "If you want to touch the shield, your best chance is to go up and touch the Wally Lewis statue out the front of the stadium, I'll take the picture, that will be the closest you get to the shield in your lifetime", after Pearce allegedly sledged him before the match saying that he was "too old for Origin".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2015-game-3-maroons-exploit-mitchell-pearce-and-paul-gallen-en-route-to-record-win/news-story/835ad75ef67f50db971738f1a4b6fb10|title=Maroons exploit Blues' weak links|date=8 July 2015|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/johnathan-thurstons-devastating-state-of-origin-putdown-to-mitchell-pearce-20150712-giagqi.html|title=Johnathan Thurston's devastating State of Origin put-down to Mitchell Pearce|first=Steve|last=Mascord|date=12 July 2015|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

On 24 September 2016, Pearce played for [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], playing at halfback in the 58–0 win in [[Port Moresby]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/mitchell-pearce-hopes-to-use-prime-ministers-xiii-game-to-push-for-australian-selection/news-story/8ce9608ed3bfe2f20a060fcf64226163|title=Pearce ready to push for Roo debut|date=16 September 2016|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=8 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/prime-ministers-xiii-smashes-papua-new-guinea-580-in-port-moresby/news-story/4a60a5a5a71753d0107dc726e0e952c1|title=Report, pictures: PM's XIII smashes PNG|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

Pearce played at halfback for New South Wales for the [[2017 State of Origin series]] in all 3 matches and scored 2 tries in another unsuccessful attempt for Pearce to be part of winning Origin team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2017-mitchell-pearce-says-somethings-wrong-with-nsw-preparation-20170722-gxgk0s.html|title=State of Origin 2017: Mitchell Pearce says 'something's wrong' with NSW preparation|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=22 July 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/has-mitchell-pearce-played-his-last-state-of-origin-game-20170712-gxa1mf.html|title=State of Origin 2017, Game Three: Has Mitchell Pearce played his last State of Origin game?|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=12 July 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

Recalled for the final game of the [[2019 State of Origin series]], Pearce was a part of New South Wales' 26–20 win and the series victory. On the 8th attempt, it was the first time Pearce had been part of a winning series.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-07-11/mitchell-pearce-redemption-james-tedesco-origin-folklore/11298468|title=State of Origin win hands Mitchell Pearce redemption as James Tedesco etches his name in folklore |publisher=ABC News|location=Australia |date=10 July 2019 |access-date=11 July 2019}}</ref>

==Controversies==
===2005 golf function incident===
In December 2005, Pearce, who was then 16 years old, was at a charity golf function in the [[Sunshine Coast, Queensland|Sunshine Coast]] when he was attacked by a drunken [[Craig Gower]], the [[Penrith Panthers]] captain. Gower allegedly chased Pearce around the resort with a glass bottle before vomiting on him; Gower was also accused of manhandling and groping Pearce's older sister Tatum Pearce.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/board-backing-gower-despite-assault-evidence/2006/01/02/1136050394672.html|title=Board backing Gower despite assault evidence – League – Sport – smh.com.au|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=3 January 2006 |access-date=9 March 2018}}</ref>

===2014 King Cross nightclub===
In May 2014, Pearce was fined $20,000 and suspended for one game by the Roosters following an incident in a [[Kings Cross, New South Wales|Kings Cross]] nightclub.  A video broadcast on the [[Nine Network]] appeared to show a woman in a yellow dress becoming agitated by Pearce's actions.  She had made a complaint to the police, but did not proceed with further action. The police issued an infringement notice to Pearce for failing to leave the club when asked and banned him from the Kings Cross area for 48 hours.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/mitchell-pearce-in-kings-cross-woman-in-yellow-dress-identifies-herself-20140513-zrb92.html|title=Mitchell Pearce in Kings Cross: Woman in yellow dress identifies herself|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=13 May 2014|access-date=27 January 2016}}</ref>

===2016 Australia Day party===
On 27 January 2016, a video was aired showing an intoxicated Pearce forcing an unwanted kiss on a woman and, when rejected, simulating sex with her dog, after having urinated on himself and her couch.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.9news.com.au/national/2016/01/27/18/51/video-emerges-of-mitchell-pearce-simulating-lewd-act-on-a-dog|title=NRL superstar Mitchell Pearce's future in doubt after video emerges of lewd act at Australia Day party|publisher=9news|date=27 January 2016|access-date=27 January 2016}}</ref> Within hours the Sydney Roosters released a short statement stating that they had advised the NRL's Integrity Unit of the incident and would conduct an internal investigation. The NRL, in turn, asked the Roosters for a report into the incident.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nothingbutleague.com/2016/01/27/club-statement/|title=Club Statement|publisher=nothingbutleague.com|date=27 January 2016|access-date=27 January 2016}}</ref> Two days after the video was first aired, an emotional Pearce read a statement to the media apologising for his actions and acknowledging that he has a problem with alcohol. He left Australia shortly thereafter, for treatment in an "intensive rehabilitation facility for an extended period".<ref>{{cite news|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/2016/01/29/19/38/pearce-roosters-drug-use|title=NRL investigates possible Roosters drug use|publisher=Channel Nine Wide World of Sports|date=29 January 2016|access-date=29 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130085208/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/2016/01/29/19/38/pearce-roosters-drug-use|archive-date=30 January 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-01-29/mitchell-pearce-entering-rehabilitation-in-overseas-facility/7126348|title=Mitchell Pearce entering intensive rehabilitation facility overseas after admitting to alcohol problem|publisher=ABC News|date=29 January 2016|access-date=29 January 2016}}</ref>

Following his return to Australia at the end of February, Pearce said that he had given up alcohol.<ref name="ABC given up">{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-02-29/mitchell-pearce-has-given-up-alcohol/7207718|title=Mitchell Pearce: Sydney Roosters star says he has given up alcohol after rehabilitation|publisher=ABC News|date=29 February 2016|access-date=29 February 2016}}</ref> Shortly afterwards, Pearce overcame the impasse in negotiations between the Roosters and the NRL over the severity of his punishment by phoning his club and saying that he "deserved to be sidelined for a lengthy period".<ref name="smh impasse">{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/how-mitchell-pearce-broke-impasse-between-roosters-and-nrl-with-one-phone-call-20160303-gn9q6i.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=How Mitchell Pearce broke impasse between Roosters and NRL with one phone call|date=3 March 2016|access-date=3 March 2016}}</ref> He was given a fine of $125,000, with $50,000 being suspended for two years, and an eight match ban.<ref name="smh impasse"/>

===Wedding postponement===
In December 2020, Pearce's wedding was reportedly called off due to him sending text messages of a sexual nature to a female Newcastle staff member. Pearce's partner had reportedly also seen the text messages.  Pearce spoke to the media about the wedding being postponed saying "It’s COVID mate … and it’s private", Pearce said.{{cn|date=December 2024}}

"We’re sorting things out. It’s a really stressful time for us. It’s our business, no-one else’s".  Newcastle media manager Frank Barrett told The Daily Telegraph: "The Newcastle Knights are aware of the issue but it’s a private matter that stays between the two individuals".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/knights/nrl-news-2020-mitchell-pearce-wedding-called-off-sexting-scandal-fiancee-kristin-scott-instagram/news-story/62b57f8b729ddabae5b4eca1297665ea|title=Mitchell Pearce and Kristin Scott's wedding reportedly called off amid allegations of sexting|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=27 December 2020}}</ref>
